summary: Manual Ableton smoke of live-stamp-write (dev mode): 'Apply to Ableton' DID write named clips into the live Arrangement at the correct beats (core mechanism works). But every clip is 1 beat long, so AbleSet only shows each lyric for 1 beat — lyrics must persist until the next line. Clips must extend from their beat to the START OF THE NEXT stamp (last clip = DEFAULT_CLIP_LENGTH), exactly as the .als export already does (als-writer.ts:218).

criteria_results.text: Apply writes named clips into the Arrangement at correct beats
criteria_results.notes: Confirmed live: clips appear at correct beats with correct names; OSC probe + in-app Apply both worked.

criteria_results.text: Clips span to the next lyric so AbleSet displays each lyric until the next line
criteria_results.notes: Clips are hardcoded 1-beat (writeStampClip create_clip(...,1.0); stampsToClips returns no length). AbleSet only shows a lyric while its clip is active, so 1-beat clips make lyrics vanish after 1 beat. The .als export path already lengths clips correctly (als-writer.ts:218: end = next beat, last = DEFAULT_CLIP_LENGTH=4) — live-apply must match.

process_issues: Planner explicitly deferred clip-length-to-next-stamp as a follow-up ('Arrangement clip = 1 beat for now'); manual smoke promoted it to a required behavior. Lesson: when an export path already encodes a correctness-critical length, the live path that replaces it must match it, not defer it.
Repair done in-process (not via a separate diagnostic agent) because root cause was fully determined from the smoke note + als-writer reference.
